human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for CPG brand-operations specificity, authority-source quality, lifecycle-state clarity, co-manufacturer and supplier boundary handling, traceability discipline, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to CPG brand execution, co-manufacturer coordination, lot and inventory traceability, quality-hold handling, blocked-release escalation, and completion evidence.

Authority sources cover FDA preventive controls and supplier-program guidance, current eCFR cGMP requirements, FDA traceability timing and scope references, FDA recall guidance, GS1 traceability and item-data standards, and CPSC non-food recall boundaries.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope execution, missing prerequisites, engineering boundary rejection, traceability conflicts, quality holds, unsupported completion, low-confidence handling, conflicting-policy escalation, supplier substitution pressure, and tenant-specific adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/consumer-packaged-goods/cpg-brand-operations-specialist/ so brand operations remain distinct from adjacent quality, planning, procurement, supplier-quality, inventory, and logistics lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around engineering or formulation approval, supplier approval, final QA release, recall disposition, and legal or regulatory interpretation.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated CPG brand-operations specialist for production-adjacent execution, traceability-aware readiness handling, supplier and hold escalation, and audit-ready completion support.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
